markets.question: Exact Score: FC Barcelona 3 - 2 Real Madrid CF?
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid CF, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the FC Barcelona vs. Real Madrid CF match originally scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona lead La Liga by nine points atop the table, bolstered by an unbeaten home streak at Spotify Camp Nou and five straight league wins, including a 2-0 victory over Getafe on April 25, yet Lamine Yamal's hamstring tear—confirmed April 23—sidelines the star winger for the season's remainder. Real Madrid, in mixed away form after a grueling April with just one win across competitions, suffer major blows as Éder Militão and Arda Güler join the injury list with season-ending hamstring issues, further straining a defense already missing Thibaut Courtois. Their 3-2 Supercopa loss to Barcelona in January highlights high-scoring potential in this title-deciding El Clásico on May 10; closely bunched probabilities across 1-1, 2-2, and 2-1 reflect trader uncertainty over depleted rosters and explosive attacks.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Warsaw be 18°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Warsaw Chopin Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Warsaw Chopin Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pl/warsaw/EPWA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts, updated May 7-8, project Warsaw's highest temperature on May 10 clustering around 16-19°C under a weak low-pressure trough bringing variable cloud cover, light precipitation, and moderate southerly winds of 10-15 km/h, aligning with trader-implied odds favoring 17-19°C outcomes at 11.5-12.5%. Differentiating factors include GFS's drier, sunnier bias pushing toward 19-20°C via enhanced boundary-layer mixing, versus ECMWF's emphasis on persistent stratus clouds capping peaks nearer 15-17°C; historical mid-May averages hover at 19°C per IMGW data, but current cool anomaly adds uncertainty. New 12Z model runs on May 9 and IMGW updates will clarify ahead of the daily maximum observation.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ground Zero Gaming and CTBC Flying Oyster each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Ground Zero Gaming vs CTBC Flying Oyster (BO3) - LCP Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ground Zero Gaming and CTBC Flying Oyster in the LCP Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 9 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ground Zero Gaming" if Ground Zero Gaming win the match against CTBC Flying Oyster.

This market will resolve to "CTBC Flying Oyster" if CTBC Flying Oyster win the match against Ground Zero Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: CTBC Flying Oyster enters as slight trader consensus favorite over Ground Zero Gaming in this pivotal LCP Split 2 regular season BO3, despite both teams' dismal starts—GZ languishing at 1-5 (17% series win rate, five-match losing streak) and CFO at 2-4 (40%) in the bottom half, where top-six playoffs positioning hangs in the balance. Recent head-to-head swings add tension: GZ stunned former champs CFO in Split 1 playoffs, but CFO rebounded with a win in last week's EWC qualifiers. GZ's January acquisition of ex-CFO support Orca bolsters their botlane duo with Shunn, pitting them against CFO's carry-threat Doggo and stable roster amid a meta favoring aggressive midlaners like GZ's JimieN.

markets.question: Game 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Ground Zero Gaming and CTBC Flying Oyster. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 2, or if Game 2 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ground Zero Gaming and CTBC Flying Oyster each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Selangor Red Giants top the MPL Malaysia Season 17 regular season standings with a flawless 9-0 record, including a dominant 2-0 sweep over Invictus Gaming on May 3 and a prior 2-0 win against AC Esports on April 24, showcasing superior map control, team fight execution, and roster synergy. AC Esports languishes at 2-7 after a recent 0-2 defeat to Team Rey, hampered by coordination issues and inconsistent draft picks in the double round-robin format. No roster changes or patch updates reported ahead of this BO3 rematch on May 9, positioning SRG as trader consensus favorite amid AC's uphill battle for playoff contention.

markets.question: Will Nicolai Hojgaard finish in the Top 20 at the 2026 ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic?
markets.description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player finishes in the top 20 at the 2026 ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic tournament, including ties. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If final results are not announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "No". The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

events.description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player finishes in the top 20 at the 2026 ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic tournament, including ties. eventMetadata.context_description: Aaron Rai holds a one-shot lead at 10-under par after Rounds 1 (65) and 2 (67) at the ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic, surging with four back-nine birdies Friday to anchor the top of the leaderboard and boost his top-20 lock-in probability amid trader consensus. Brooks Koepka sits T22 at 4-under, leveraging major-winning pedigree and recent PGA Tour return via expanded 123-player field, keeping him in solid contention despite missing early cuts elsewhere. Multiple pre-tournament withdrawals, including Marco Penge for health reasons, and a Round 1 weather suspension thinned competition, while Brandt Snedeker and Mark Hubbard lurk at 9-under. With Rounds 3-4 ahead at windy Dunes Golf & Beach Club, birdie opportunities and putting prowess will dictate final top-20 placements in this opposite-field event offering key FedExCup points.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 77% implied probability to no Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, driven by stalled progress since the company's confidential SEC filing in early January, which targeted a March debut but yielded no public S-1 prospectus, roadshow, or pricing signals. Secondary market valuations have halved to $7-10 billion from the $15 billion 2021 funding peak, pressured by monetization challenges in a gamer-heavy user base yielding ~$600-800 million annual recurring revenue—far below enterprise comparables like Slack's $2.5 billion. The 12% odds on <15B capture trader expectations for subdued pricing if listed, while 30B+ at 1% reflects dim growth prospects absent enterprise pivots or macro tailwinds, with focus now on potential Q3 catalysts.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Movistar KOI and G2 Esports in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 10 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Movistar KOI" if Movistar KOI win the match against G2 Esports.

This market will resolve to "G2 Esports" if G2 Esports win the match against Movistar KOI.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: G2 Esports enters this LEC Spring 2026 regular season BO3 tied with Movistar KOI at 4-2 series records, holding a marginal edge in game differential (10-4 vs. 10-5) amid tight top-six playoff positioning. G2's trader-favored status stems from their recent 2-0 sweep of third-place Natus Vincere two days ago, extending a three-win streak, while MKOI dominated bottom-dwellers Shifters (multiple 2-0/2-1 wins) and Team Heretics. Earlier season H2H saw G2 dismantle MKOI 3-0 in LEC Versus playoffs, bolstered by Caps' midlane dominance and stable rosters featuring Elyoya's jungle aggression for MKOI. Current patch emphasizes Ryze and Nautilus picks, with no reported injuries or subs.

markets.question: Jerome Powell out as Fed Chair by May 16, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell ceases to be Chair of the Federal Reserve for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Jerome Powell's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Jerome Powell out as Fed Chair by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell ceases to be the Chair of the U.S. Federal Reserve for any period of time between this market's creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the U.S. Government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires May 15, 2026, after which he has confirmed he will step down but remain on the Board of Governors through 2028, breaking historical precedent amid tensions with President Trump. Trump nominated Kevin Warsh as successor in January, with the Senate Banking Committee advancing the nomination on a party-line vote last week, setting up a full Senate confirmation vote that could occur before or after the deadline. Recent developments include Powell's final FOMC press conference on April 29—holding rates steady—and the DOJ dropping a criminal probe into Fed renovations. Traders watch Senate timing, as delays could lead to a brief acting chair period, underscoring Fed independence debates and potential policy shifts under Warsh.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be between 66-67°F on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight clustering around 64-67°F for Seattle's highest temperature on May 10, with 64-65°F leading at 33.5% amid model ensemble spread from the National Weather Service (NWS), GFS, and ECMWF. Persistent onshore flow is advecting cool, moist marine air across the Puget Sound, fostering low stratus clouds that cap diurnal heating and differentiate outcomes—earlier burn-off could nudge highs toward 66-67°F, while prolonged overcast favors 62-65°F. A weak frontal remnant from May 8 lingers, but a rebuilding upper ridge aloft offers limited warming potential against climatological May norms near 65°F. NWS guidance as of May 9 emphasizes this uncertainty; watch afternoon observations and final evening forecast update for resolution shifts based on boundary layer mixing.

markets.question: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
markets.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

events.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84.5% implied probability for "No," driven by Congressional Budget Office projections showing the fiscal year 2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion (5.8% of GDP), with year-to-date figures already exceeding $1 trillion through February amid persistent revenue shortfalls and spending pressures. President Trump's policy mix—extension of 2017 tax cuts adding $1.4 trillion to decade-long deficits, alongside 42% defense spending hikes to $1.5 trillion in the April FY2027 budget proposal—offsets proposed non-defense discretionary cuts, per CBO analysis. Recent tariff policy shifts could further widen shortfalls by $1.1 trillion over ten years, reinforcing fiscal expansion. Key catalysts include final FY2026 Treasury data by September and debt ceiling negotiations.

markets.question: Estudiantes de La Plata vs. Racing Club: O/U 3.5

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $130 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point after market creation during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours. 

Per CME contract specifications for WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, a contract’s last trading day is three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day).

The active month changes at the start of the second trading session prior to the nearest listed contract’s last trading session. At that point, the next listed contract becomes the active month (i.e., for the final three trading sessions of the nearest listed contract, the contract for the next month is the active month). The trading session for a given business day typically begins at 6:00 PM ET on the prior calendar date.

For example, if the 25th of the month is a Saturday, the last trading session for the nearest listed contract is the session for Tuesday the 21st, and the next listed contract becomes the active month at the start of the trading session for Friday the 17th (6:00 PM ET on Thursday), assuming a standard trading calendar.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

events.title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures have tumbled over 7% this week to around $95 per barrel, driven by President Trump's reaffirmation of the US-Iran ceasefire amid Strait of Hormuz tensions, easing trader fears of prolonged supply disruptions and compressing the geopolitical risk premium. OPEC+ producers including Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, and others raised output on May 3 to support market stability, countering high US production forecasts averaging 13.5 million b/d in 2026 per EIA estimates. Softening global demand signals, including Asia's economic headwinds and projected contractions of 80 kb/d, further temper upside amid widening Brent-WTI spreads. Key catalysts include tomorrow's EIA inventories and OPEC's May 13 Oil Market Report, with potential volatility through May resolution.

markets.question: Will Trump agree to Iranian transit fees in the Strait of Hormuz by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States agrees to Iran charging fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Iran charging fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz refers to U.S. acceptance of Iran imposing tolls, transit fees, passage charges, or other mandatory payments on commercial vessels in exchange for transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

The United States will be considered to have agreed to Iran charging such fees if:
- Donald Trump or another authorized representative of the Government of the United States publicly announces that the United States has definitively agreed to accept Iran charging such fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
- Iran charging such fees is included as part of a treaty or deal formally established between the United States and Iran, including through signing or other formal means.

Agreement refers to an explicit acceptance, authorization, or consent to the specified action. Only announcements of definitive agreement will qualify. Suggestions, negotiations, expressions of openness, or other non-definitive statements will not qualify.

Any definitive agreement or commitment made before the resolution date will qualify, regardless of when or whether the specified action is implemented.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Donald Trump, the U.S. government, and their official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used to verify the details of an announcement or formal agreement.
markets.groupItemTitle: Transit Fees in the Strait of Hormuz

events.title: What Iranian demands will Trump agree to by May 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States agrees to the continued enrichment of uranium by Iran by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Continued enrichment of uranium by Iran refers to US acceptance of the enrichment of, or the right to enrich, any quantity of uranium by Iran for any future amount of time. Agreements that include limitations, restrictions, or specified terms (e.g., caps on enrichment level, monitoring requirements) will qualify, provided the United States accepts continued enrichment.

The United States will be considered to have agreed to the continued enrichment of uranium by Iran if:

- Donald Trump or another authorized representative of the Government of the United States publicly announces that they have definitively agreed to accept the continued enrichment of uranium by Iran.
- Continued enrichment of uranium by Iran is included as part of a treaty or deal that is formally established between the United States and Iran, either through signing or other formal means.

Agreement refers to an explicit acceptance, authorization or consent to the specified action. Only announcements of definitive agreement will qualify. Suggestions, negotiations, expressions of openness, or other non-definitive statements will not qualify.

Any definitive agreement or commitment made before the resolution date will be considered, regardless of when or whether the specified action is begun.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Donald Trump and the US government and their official representatives; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used to verify the details of an announcement or formal agreement.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fragile ceasefire in the Strait of Hormuz faces new tests from U.S. strikes on Iranian tankers and fresh sanctions announced Friday, tempering optimism despite President Trump's reports of "very good talks" with Iran over the past 24 hours. Tehran is reviewing a U.S. proposal from May 6 that sidesteps core demands in its 14-point response, including oil sanction relief, unfrozen assets, troop withdrawal, and transit fees, leaving diplomatic sticking points unresolved. Traders reflect this uncertainty with competitive implied probabilities for limited concessions like asset releases by May 31, viewing acceptance of uranium enrichment or Hormuz fees as improbable amid Trump's maximum pressure strategy. Potential talks in Islamabad next week could shift dynamics before the deadline.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT May 8 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the May 9 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT May 8 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the May 9 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-12 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Democratic nominee Julie Fortier advanced unopposed but faces steep historical margins, with Bost securing comfortable wins in prior cycles amid limited recent polling or fundraising shifts. Absent major catalysts like a late scandal, health issue, or national Democratic wave boosting turnout in battleground areas, the odds reflect incumbency advantages and structural electoral math favoring the GOP ahead of the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize remains fragmented in a wide-open field, with Yulia Navalnaya's 7.5% implied probability narrowly leading Donald Trump (6.5%) and Volodymyr Zelenskyy (5.5%), reflecting the Norwegian Nobel Committee's opaque voting and history of upsets favoring underdogs. The April 30 Reuters disclosure of 287 nominees—likely including Trump amid his diplomatic deal-making buzz—sparked recent momentum shifts, elevating speculation on his Abraham Accords legacy, while Navalnaya embodies anti-authoritarian resistance post-Navalny, Zelenskyy Ukraine's defensive stand, UNRWA Gaza aid efforts, and Pope Leo XIV's Vatican peace advocacy. Differentiators include human rights narratives versus geopolitical brokering; with the October 10 announcement looming, breakthroughs in ongoing conflicts could dramatically alter frontrunner dynamics.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the WY-AL House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WY-AL congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Qingdao Jiaodong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/qingdao/ZSQD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: O/U 1.5 Rounds
markets.description:
Each market will resolve to “Over” if the fight between Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, lasts beyond the listed round threshold.

Each market will resolve to “Under” if the fight between Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, does not last beyond the listed round threshold.

For example:

“Over 1.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight continues past the 2:30 mark of Round 2.

“Under 1.5 Rounds” will resolve “Yes” if the fight ends before the 2:30 mark of Round 2.

If the fight ends exactly at the threshold (e.g., 2:30 of Round 2 for a 1.5 line), it will resolve “50-50.”

If the bout is not scored, postponed beyond May 23, 2026, or canceled, all options will resolve “50-50.”

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

Both hit the middleweight limit at yesterday's weigh-ins, officially confirming the bout despite Chimaev appearing drained and dejected—commentators noted his labored posture reminiscent of prior tough cuts—while Strickland looked sharp in the staredown. Tensions boiled over two days ago at the press conference when Chimaev kicked Strickland's lower leg, igniting a melee that necessitated heavy police presence through fight week. Trader consensus reflects Chimaev's grappling pressure and undefeated streak versus Strickland's high-volume boxing and proven Octagon durability, with no late injuries or scratches reported ahead of the five-round war.

markets.question: Will Russia capture Rai-Oleksandrivka by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the intersection at 48.810403° N, 37.851396° E in Rai-Oleksandrivka, Donetsk Oblast, by the specified date 11:59 PM ET.

The intersection will be considered captured if any part of the intersection is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the specified date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the intersection, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Intersection Location:  https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Rai-Oleksandrivka1.png 

Intersection Location in Rai-Oleksandrivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Rai-Oleksandrivka2.png 

Rai-Oleksandrivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Rai-Oleksandrivka3.png

Location on Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/98NiyN9BC3XQfy3N7

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Austin Scott commands 93% trader consensus in the GA-08 Republican primary due to his 15-year tenure in the solidly Republican district (R+15 Cook PVI), history of unopposed or dominant primaries—including 2024—and superior fundraising with over $1.3 million cash on hand as of late March. Challenger Vinson Watkins, a perennial low-fundraiser who raised just $5,000 in his 2024 Democratic primary bid here (losing 28%-72%), trails at 4.7% amid reports of his withdrawal or disqualification, leaving Scott effectively unopposed ahead of the May 19 primary. Udinese sit comfortably 11th with 43 points (12W-7D-14L, GD -5), buoyed by a stunning 3-0 away win over AC Milan on April 11 but dented by a 0-1 home loss to Parma last weekend. Cagliari's recent form shows a vital 1-0 home victory over Cremonese sandwiched between defeats to Sassuolo and Inter, while injuries sideline forward Leonardo Pavoletti (physical discomfort) and winger Riyad Idrissi (cruciate ligament tear); Udinese miss Keinan Davis, Jordan Zemura, and Alessandro Zanoli. Home advantage and desperation could fuel Cagliari, but Udinese hold a strong head-to-head edge, including a 1-1 draw in October.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the MS-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MS-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Bennie Thompson's decisive March 10 primary win over challengers Evan Turnage and Pertis Williams III has reinforced trader consensus favoring a Democratic hold on Mississippi's 2nd Congressional District, a solidly Democratic seat rated as such by the Cook Political Report with his service dating to 1993. Republican nominee Ron Eller, who prevailed in the GOP primary, faces steep historical odds in the majority-minority district ahead of the November 3 general election. Recent pressure from former President Trump and state House Speaker Jason White's May 7 announcement of a redistricting study committee—prompted by a late-April Supreme Court ruling easing Voting Rights Act constraints—have sparked speculation on map changes, yet procedural barriers and timing constraints limit near-term impact, keeping Democratic probabilities at 85.5%.

markets.question: Will there be between 0 and 10 average daily transits of the Strait of Hormuz on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz that IMF Portwatch reports for May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data for the specified date has been published. If no data for the specified date has been published by June 14, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on data for the most recent date prior to May 31, 2026, for which data is available.

This market will resolve based on the first publication of data for May 31, 2026. Any subsequent revisions will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors severely curtailed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz end of May, with 0-10 ships (37.5%) and 10-20 (26.5%) leading as the 7-day moving average of IMF Portwatch transit calls lingers in single to low double digits amid the ongoing US-Iran crisis. Recent military escalations, including Iranian missile launches toward US ships on May 8 and US strikes on Iranian tankers testing blockades, have dashed hopes for normalization despite limited successes under the US-led Project Freedom escorts, which enabled just a handful of transits like two American-flagged vessels last week. Persistent mutual blockades and stalled ceasefire talks signal continued low traffic, far below pre-February averages of over 100 vessels daily, with diplomacy or de-escalation needed to shift odds higher.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy enters the BO3 matchup against Wave Esports in Exort Series #25 Main Stage Swiss as the higher-pedigree favorite, backed by Team Spirit's academy infrastructure and a refreshed roster featuring rifler Kiryasoo (0.98 HLTV rating) alongside newcomers VILBy, mazay, kidofpain, and s1nside. Wave (#251 HLTV), hampered by recent stand-ins (cMS for coldpera, pxsl for B3LOF) and a 37.5% win rate over the last three months—including sweeps in Urban Riga Open Season 3—struggles with consistency on the map pool. No major developments in the past 30 days; trader consensus hinges on Spirit's depth for T-side executes and pistol rounds versus Wave's upset potential through individual frags in this elimination-style Swiss round.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

Realistic challenges include data revisions from sources like UNCTAD or Windward revealing undercounted transits, potentially pushing totals toward 50+, though current positioning shows strong skin-in-the-game alignment.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in forecast models for São Paulo's highest temperature on May 11, with implied probabilities tightly clustered at 37.5% for 20°C, 37.0% for 18°C, and 36.0% for 21°C, driven by an advancing cold front from the South. INMET's May 4-11 weekly outlook indicates mostly stable weather in the Southeast with low cloudiness and possible weak showers in southern São Paulo, but the front's timing could introduce southerly winds, increased cloud cover, and rain that suppress peak daytime heating below May climatological averages of 22-23°C. Model ensembles diverge on frontal passage speed—earlier arrival favors 18-19°C maxima via cooler air advection, while delays allow 20-21°C—pending tomorrow's updated INMET and CPTEC guidance. Urban heat island effects at official stations like Mirante de Santana add minor variability to observations.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between The Soulless Team and Kings Crown in the BetBoom Rise of Legends 2nd Division, initially scheduled for May 4 at 12:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "The Soulless Team" if The Soulless Team win the match against Kings Crown.

This market will resolve to "Kings Crown" if Kings Crown win the match against The Soulless Team.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the BetBoom Rise of Legends Season 10 2nd Division double round-robin group stage, The Soulless Team co-leads standings at 4-1 match record (9-2 games, 13 points) after a clean 2-0 sweep of Aterion Esports on May 1, building on four straight prior wins marred only by a narrow 1-2 loss to Dark Phoenix on April 30. Kings Crown sits seventh at 2-3 (4-7 games, 5 points), salvaging momentum with a 2-1 comeback over The World on May 1 but hampered by 0-2 defeats to top contenders HARD GRIND, Team Spirit Academy White, and Aterion. TST boasts perfect win rates on key meta heroes like Khaleed, Harley, and Sora, while KC struggles on picks such as Zhuxin and Kalea; this BO3 clash carries weight for closed qualifier seeding in the B-Tier circuit.

markets.question: Will the Eurozone's 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.6% and 1.8%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the Eurozone over the 12-month period ending December 2026 as reported by Eurostat.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly Eurostat report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone HICP inflation surged to 3.0% in April 2026 per flash estimates, up from 2.6% in March and fueled by a 10.9% energy price spike amid Mideast war risks, driving Polymarket traders to price 3.1%+ annual 2026 inflation at 57.5% implied probability as the consensus outcome. The ECB's second-quarter Survey of Professional Forecasters revised 2026 average inflation upward to 2.7% from prior estimates, with GDP growth cut to 1.0%, signaling sticky price pressures that temper rate cut expectations despite the 2% target. Corporate polls highlight surge risks if conflicts persist, positioning 2.8-3.0% (23.1%) as the next likely tier, while sub-2% outcomes reflect lower conviction amid recent upward momentum; full April data due May 20 could refine trader sentiment.

markets.question: Will Czechia win on 2026-06-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 11, 2026
If Czechia wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Korea Republic vs. Czechia
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Thursday, June 11, 2026 between Korea Republic and Czechia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Korea Republic a slim 34.5% favorite over Czechia at 34.0% with draw at 31.0%, capturing the razor-thin dynamics of this FIFA World Cup Group A opener at neutral Estadio Akron in high-altitude Guadalajara. Czechia's momentum from their March playoff triumph over Denmark via penalties contrasts South Korea's back-to-back friendly losses to Ivory Coast and Austria, fostering uncertainty despite Korea's higher 25th FIFA ranking versus Czechia's 41st. Limited head-to-head history leans toward draws, while potential absences like South Korea's Hwang In-beom and Jae-Sung Lee alongside Czechia's Ladislav Krejcí add volatility to lineups heavy on stars like Son Heung-min and Tomas Soucek. Both sides eye crucial early points against a Mexico-led group.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the Republican Party at 80% to win South Carolina's 2nd Congressional District House seat, reflecting the district's R+7 partisan lean, Donald Trump's 14-point 2024 margin, and incumbent Joe Wilson's fundraising dominance with nearly $300,000 cash-on-hand versus underfunded Democratic primary contenders like Zyon Khalifa and David Robinson II. Wilson's past general election victory by 60% and primary by 74% reinforce GOP solidity ahead of the November 3 general election. Tight odds near 50% on candidates A, B, and Other capture pre-primary volatility in the June 9 Republican primary, where Wilson faces challengers Sam Gibbons and Hamp Redmond. Recent GOP-led efforts in the South Carolina House to delay primaries amid redistricting have introduced minor uncertainty but left SC-02 odds stable.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors retired Army Maj. Gen. Bert Mizusawa as the Republican nominee for Virginia's U.S. Senate primary on August 4, 2026, reflecting his slight edge in a May 1-5 Public Sentiment Institute poll (23.5%) over Kim Farington (22.3%) and David Williams (10.1%), amid low general election prospects against incumbent Mark Warner. Mizusawa's military credentials and national security focus position him as the strongest general election contender in this open primary field, certified by the Republican Party of Virginia on April 7 with these three leading alongside minor candidates. The closely contested race leaves room for endorsements, fundraising surges, or debates to shift dynamics before early voting begins June 18.

markets.question: Bencic vs. Kalinskaya: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Belinda Bencic and Anna Kalinskaya in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA results.

events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Belinda Bencic and Anna Kalinskaya in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 9, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Belinda Bencic' if Belinda Bencic advances against Anna Kalinskaya.

This market will resolve to 'Anna Kalinskaya' if Anna Kalinskaya advances against Belinda Bencic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Belinda Bencic enters her third-round matchup at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia with a commanding 4-0 head-to-head edge over Anna Kalinskaya, including a straight-sets win in their latest encounter at Adelaide 2025, fueling trader consensus at 61% implied probability on the 12th seed. Bencic advanced with a gritty comeback against Bianca Andreescu yesterday, winning 20 of the final 22 points on clay, while Kalinskaya, the 22nd seed, grinded out a three-set thriller over Katerina Siniakova. Both showed solid early clay form post-Madrid exits—Bencic falling to Hailey Baptiste, Kalinskaya to Dalma Galfi—but Bencic's all-court versatility and surface history give her the matchup edge on Rome's red clay, despite Kalinskaya's rising power baseline game.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in London be 4°C or below on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a lowest temperature of 9°C in London on May 9, reflecting the latest Met Office and BBC Weather forecasts updated this morning, which pinpoint a minimum nighttime temperature of 9°C amid stable high-pressure conditions and light winds limiting radiative cooling. Early morning observations at key sites like Heathrow and Battersea Heliport align closely with this, showing overnight lows around 9°C under partly cloudy skies. Model consensus from the UK Met Office indicates mild southerly flows preventing sharper drops, consistent with May climatology where average lows hover near 9°C. Realistic challenges include unexpected prolonged clear skies overnight fostering greater cooling to 8°C or below, though current guidance shows low likelihood; final observations will resolve by midnight.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Darth Vader" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Irish Times/TG4/Ipsos B&A polling from early May shows Fine Gael's Seán Kyne holding a slim 17%-16% first-preference lead over Independent Ireland's Noel Thomas in the May 22 Galway West Dáil by-election, with Labour's Helen Ogbu at 12% in a fragmented 17-candidate field under PR-STV where transfers will decide the single seat vacated by President Catherine Connolly. Trader consensus nevertheless prices Thomas as narrow favorite at 46.5% implied probability versus Kyne's 37.7%, driven by anti-government sentiment on housing shortages, the Galway bypass, cost-of-living pressures, and fuel protests boosting Independent Ireland's appeal despite Kyne's name recognition as former chief whip. The race stays tight amid unpredictable lower-preference flows and undecided voters; upcoming debates, final polls, and registration deadline could tip balances through turnout or pacts among left-leaning candidates.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
